name: Estarreja and Vouga Valley Ecotrail Loop
name_original: De Estarreja à Ponte do poço de Santiago na Ecopista do Vouga — percurso circular
description: This difficult 42.4-mile touring cycling route on the Ecopista do Vouga offers varied terrain and historical sights from Estarreja.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Beduído benefit from the region's flat terrain, making it suitable for relaxed rides. The area is characterized by its proximity to the Ria de Aveiro, a unique lagoon ecosystem with extensive cycle paths. These routes often incorporate scenic coastal sections and well-maintained ecotrails, providing varied cycling experiences through marshlands and along the water. Best touring cycling routes around Beduído The most popular touring cycling route is Estarreja and Vouga Valley Ecotrail Loop, a 42.4 miles (68.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the ecotrail and the Vouga Valley. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Great Route of Ria de Aveiro – Boardwalk in Ria de Aveiro loop from Estarreja, a moderate 32.9 miles (52.9 km) path. This route features a boardwalk section through the Ria de Aveiro. Local touring cyclists also love the Cais da Bestida – View of Ria de Aveiro loop from Estarreja, a 23.0 miles (37.1 km) trail leading through the Ria de Aveiro, often completed in about 2 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Beduído is defined by the flat terrain of the Ria de Aveiro, extensive cycle paths, and coastal routes. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes. The routes in Beduído are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 700 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Beduído's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Beduído?
A: There are over 50 touring cycling routes around Beduído, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 70 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Beduído?
A: The terrain around Beduído is predominantly flat, making it ideal for relaxed touring cycling. Routes often follow the unique ecosystem of the Ria de Aveiro, with extensive cycle paths through marshlands, along canals, and sometimes incorporating scenic coastal sections. This flat landscape is a significant advantage for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes suitable for beginners around Beduído?
A: Yes, Beduído and the wider Aveiro region offer many easy and family-friendly routes due to the flat terrain. There are 18 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10407670" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike loop from Salreu&lt;/a&gt;, which is just under 5 miles (7.8 km) long and features minimal elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the Beduído area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Beduído are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14878069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cais da Bestida – View of Ria de Aveiro loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 23.0 miles (37.1 km) ride through the Ria de Aveiro.

Q: What natural features and landmarks can I explore while cycling around Beduído?
A: While cycling around Beduído, you can explore the stunning Ria de Aveiro, a unique lagoon ecosystem. Highlights include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528323" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Canal 🌊 Aveiro Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;EV1 on the Ria de Aveiro&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of a major European cycle route. You might also encounter charming towns like Ovar, with its &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2924364" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ovar Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;, and various bridges like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4719493" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcavelos Bridge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any coastal touring cycling routes near Beduído?
A: Yes, Beduído's proximity to the Atlantic coastline means you can enjoy scenic coastal routes. These often include sections along beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Areinho Beach&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3130637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagoon beach ⛱️&lt;/a&gt;, providing beautiful ocean views and opportunities to visit sandy shores. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/528328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torreira, Ria de Aveiro 🌊 CicloRia EuroVelo 1&lt;/a&gt; is another excellent example of a route combining lagoon and coastal elements.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in the Aveiro region?
A: The Aveiro region, including Beduído, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring (April-June) and early autumn (September-October) are often considered ideal, with comfortable temperatures and less rainfall. Summers can be warm, but coastal breezes can make cycling enjoyable. Winters are mild but may have more rainy days.

Q: What is the most challenging touring cycling route around Beduído?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1384975703/estarreja-and-vouga-valley-ecotrail-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estarreja and Vouga Valley Ecotrail Loop&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult. This 42.4 miles (68.2 km) route takes approximately 4 hours 50 minutes to complete and explores the ecotrail and the Vouga Valley, featuring significant elevation changes compared to other routes in the area.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes that feature boardwalk sections?
A: Yes, some routes in the Beduído area incorporate unique boardwalk sections, particularly those traversing the Ria de Aveiro. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10408237" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Great Route of Ria de Aveiro – Boardwalk in Ria de Aveiro loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 32.9 miles (52.9 km) path specifically known for its boardwalk experience through the lagoon.

Q: Can I find less crowded touring cycling routes around Beduído?
A: While popular routes can see more activity, the extensive network of cycle paths and ecotrails in the wider Aveiro region means it's often possible to find quieter sections. Exploring routes further from the main towns or during off-peak hours can lead to a more serene cycling experience. The region's focus on sustainable mobility, like Murtosa's 'NaturRia' network, ensures many well-maintained paths are available for exploration.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Beduído?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the flat terrain, which makes for enjoyable and accessible rides, the unique natural beauty of the Ria de Aveiro, and the well-maintained cycle paths. The opportunity to explore charming towns and observe diverse birdlife also contributes to a highly positive experience.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that lead to a lagoon beach?
A: Yes, you can cycle to beautiful lagoon beaches in the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32190067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cais da Bestida – Lagoon beach ⛱️ loop from Estarreja&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 40.4 miles (64.9 km) route that specifically leads to a lagoon beach, offering a pleasant destination for a break.
tips:
Estarreja is known for the Ria de Aveiro, the annual Carnival and Foral do Antuã of D. Manuel I and for its unique urban art.

At the ESTAU Festival, started by the artist Bordallo II, artists from all over the world transform the city with murals and installations inspired by local culture. In the link below you can find all the murals in Estarreja:
https://www.cm-estarreja.pt/estau

Pinheiro da Bemposta, is located between the Antuã and Caima rivers. Its history goes back to D. Teresa, mother of D. Afonso Henriques. The Cruzeiro do Pinheiro da Bemposta, right in the center of the village, and the Chapel of Nossa Senhora da Ribeira stand out. Pinheiro da Bemposta is on the Portuguese route to Santiago.

The Paradela Railway Station served the towns of Sever do Vouga and Paradela. Opened in 1913, the station closed in 1990 and subsequently fell into ruin. In 2011, Sociedade Polis Litoral Ria de Aveiro began its recovery, transforming it into a support hub for the Linha do Vouga eco-trail. Today it offers changing rooms, bicycle rental and repair, an environmental interpretation center, restaurant and terrace, making it a strategic stopping point for cyclists traveling through the region.

The Poço de S. Tiago Bridge, built between 1912 and 1913, is a remarkable 165-meter engineering work along the Vouga River. Integrated into the Vale do Vouga Line, closed in 1990, it was converted into an eco-track, revitalizing the old Paradela station as a tourist attraction.

For cyclists on road bikes: be careful, the bridge is passable, but the surface is gravel. It does not present significant technical difficulties, but it is important to take this into account.

Albergaria-a-Velha has a long tradition associated with pilgrimage (hence the name) and is part of the current Portuguese route to Santiago de Compostela. The central square is charming and an ideal stopping point on a bike ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is designed for touring bicycles and is considered difficult. It covers approximately 68 kilometers with significant elevation gain, so plan for around 4 hours and 50 minutes of cycling time. This duration doesn't include stops for rest or sightseeing.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Ecopista do Vouga circular route from Estarreja?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging. While much of the route is asphalt (about 64 km) and paved, there are also unpaved sections, gravel, and ground surfaces. Expect considerable climbing and a demanding ride, requiring very good fitness for road cycling.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and historical landmarks. Highlights include the Urban Art Murals of Estarreja, the charming settlement of Pinheiro da Bemposta, and the historic Paradela Station, which often hosts an eco-cafe. The iconic Poço de S. Tiago Bridge is a breathtaking architectural landmark, and you can also see the Statue of Queen D. Teresa in Albergaria-a-Velha.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: No, this specific circular route is rated as difficult and requires very good fitness due to its significant elevation gain and varied terrain, including unpaved sections. It is not recommended for beginners or families with young children.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ecopista do Vouga is generally pleasant in spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be hot, and winter might bring rain. Always check the local weather forecast before you go, especially considering the route's length and exposure.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail, especially since it's in a Special Protection Zone?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. While parts of the area are within the Zona de Proteção Especial da Ria de Aveiro (Special Protection Zone of Ria de Aveiro), public access to the Ecopista do Vouga for cycling is generally free. However, always respect local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Ecopista do Vouga generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash at all times, especially since parts of the route pass through natural areas and residential zones. Be mindful of other cyclists and pedestrians.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access public transport near the trailhead in Estarreja?
faqs.answer: Estarreja is a good starting point. You can usually find public parking in the town. For public transport, Estarreja has a train station, making it accessible by rail, which can be convenient for cyclists looking to start their journey directly from the town.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ecopista do Vale do Vouga, the Portuguese Way of St. James, and the Caminhos de Fátima. Smaller sections also overlap with the Grande Rota das Montanhas Mágicas and the Rota Verde ferroviária.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or rest along the Ecopista do Vouga?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Ecopista do Vouga is known for its support structures. You'll find eco-cafes at old train stations, such as the Paradela Station, where you can rest and recharge. Estarreja and other settlements along the broader route also offer various cafes and restaurants.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect while cycling this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers picturesque scenery, following the course of the Vouga River through green landscapes, pine forests, oak groves, and native forests. You'll experience the tranquility of the river, the exuberance of nature, and pass through rural and residential areas, often encountering historical landmarks and unique railway tunnels.

waypoints.name: Statue of Queen D. Teresa in Albergaria-a-Velha
